evals.prompt: Using nextjs-feature-architecture, add a comments feature for blog posts: list comments on a post and let users submit new ones. We use Prisma and the App Router. Post page is app/posts/[id]/page.tsx.
evals.expected_output: Architecture skeleton (Topology + Architecture sections) before code; features/comments/ slice with schemas, services, repositories, actions; server page loads comments; no useEffect fetch on page.
expectations: Response includes ## Topology before substantial code blocks
Response includes ## Architecture with features/comments/ path
Does not recommend use client on the post page for initial comment loading
Mentions Server Action or actions/ for create comment mutation
Mentions Zod or schemas/ for validation

evals.prompt: Using nextjs-feature-architecture, refactor app/dashboard/page.tsx — it's a client component that useEffect-fetches stats from /api/dashboard. Move to server-first feature slice.
evals.expected_output: Refactor plan with skeleton first; async server dashboard page; get-dashboard.service.ts; small client island only for interactive filters if needed; remove redundant internal API route if only used for this fetch.
expectations: Response includes architecture skeleton sections before implementation code
Proposes features/dashboard/ or similar feature folder
Does not keep use client on page.tsx for data fetching
Mentions service layer for the fetch logic

evals.prompt: Using nextjs-feature-architecture, add an item detail page at app/items/[id]/page.tsx with server-rendered data from Connect RPC (proto @acme/api). Users should be able to refresh item data on the client without full page reload.
evals.expected_output: Topology Separate-gRPC; lib/grpc/clients.ts; ServiceResult in service; server page initial load; TanStack via item.queries.ts server bridge; hook does not import grpc client directly.
expectations: Mentions lib/grpc/clients.ts or single client registry
Mentions queries.ts or server action bridge for client refresh
Hook or TanStack queryFn does not import ApiClient directly
Mentions ServiceResult or success/error result shape
